Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
All laboratory values are within normal limits except for a mildly elevated LDL cholesterol (113.3 mg/dL; normal <100 mg/dL). Glycemic control, renal function, electrolytes, and urine microalbumin are normal. Recommend addressing LDL elevation with lifestyle modification and/or medication as clinically indicated, considering cardiovascular risk factors. No acute laboratory concerns.
